content:
- Read the current task summary, completed task summary, and the previous Choose-Task progress log.
- Confirmed there are many active failing tasks and at least one completed high-priority bug in done tasks.
- Next: refresh the Ralph summary, inspect active task files for blocker and priority tags, then select the highest-precedence unblocked task.

- Refreshed the Ralph task summaries and inspected all active task files for blocker and priority tags.
- Confirmed there are no active failing bug tasks, no active meta-task, and only one blocked active task: story-22a task 02 is blocked by story-22a task 01.
- Chose story-18 verify-image task 02 as the next task because story 18 task 01 is already complete, task 02 is unblocked, and it is the most logical core implementation step in the active three-image split chain.
- Next if resuming after a context cut: keep `.ralph/current_task.txt` pointed at story-18 task 02 and start that task with the tdd skill.

- Wrote `.ralph/current_task.txt` with `.ralph/tasks/story-18-verify-http-image/02-task-upgrade-the-verify-slice-to-go-1-26-and-bump-all-dependencies.md`.
- Selection is final for this turn: no active failing bug outranked it, no active priority tag overrode it, and blocked task story-22a/02 was excluded correctly.
- Next after quitting: begin story-18 task 02 with TDD and keep validating `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint`.

- Read startup state: progress log, current task pointer, and required skill files for improve-code-boundaries and tdd.
- Current task file is .ralph/tasks/story-18-verify-http-image/02-task-upgrade-the-verify-slice-to-go-1-26-and-bump-all-dependencies.md.
- Next step: inspect the task file and its plan state to determine whether this turn is planning-only or execution.

- Read the current task file; it has no plan reference or NOW EXECUTE marker, so this turn should create a plan and stop.
- Next step: inspect an existing task/plan pair in the same story to mirror the expected plan structure and status markers.

- Read an existing completed task and plan in the same story to mirror Ralph formatting.
- Confirmed the current task needs a new plan file plus a <plan> link in the task file before any execution work.
- Next step: inspect current verify-slice module and existing contract tests so the plan is specific about toolchain/dependency checks and code-boundary cleanup.

- Inspected current go.mod and discovered the verify slice is still on `go 1.21`.
- Found that task 01 already added `crates/runner/tests/support/verify_source_contract.rs`, so task 02 should extend that existing contract boundary for toolchain/dependency modernization.
- Next step: inspect that support module plus Go-related references in workflow/Makefile to write a specific execution plan.

- Confirmed the Rust CI contract already owns verify-slice source assertions; task 02 should extend that support module rather than add a separate parallel contract boundary.
- Current build lanes do not invoke Go directly from Makefile, so the plan must keep repo-contract checks Rust-based while also scheduling local Go sanity commands during execution.
- Next step: inspect direct imports in the retained Go slice so the dependency-upgrade plan can target real used packages and stale transitive baggage.

- Mapped the retained verify-slice import surface to ground the plan in actual used dependencies instead of a generic `go get -u` style upgrade.
- Spotted a likely code-boundary cleanup opportunity around duplicate error packages; checking whether `github.com/pkg/errors` can be removed while upgrading.
- Next step: finish that smell check and write the task-02 plan file plus task <plan> reference.

- Wrote the task-02 execution plan at .ralph/tasks/story-18-verify-http-image/02-task-upgrade-the-verify-slice-to-go-1-26-and-bump-all-dependencies_plans/2026-04-19-verify-go-1-26-dependency-upgrade-plan.md.
- Linked that plan from the task file and marked the end of the plan as NOW EXECUTE.
- Key execution focus for the next turn: extend VerifySourceContract for Go 1.26 + manifest assertions, remove the duplicate pkg/errors boundary, refresh go.mod/go.sum, then run local Go validation followed by make check/lint/test/test-long.

- Read startup state, current task pointer, current task file, and the linked plan.
- Confirmed the task is in NOW EXECUTE and loaded the required tdd and improve-code-boundaries skills.
- Inspected the current verify contract boundary, go.mod, and mysqlconv duplicate-error site; go.mod still says go 1.21 and mysqlconv still imports github.com/pkg/errors.
- Noted local toolchain state: host has go1.25.5, so Go 1.26 validation may require bootstrapping a newer toolchain during execution.
- Next: implement the first RED slice in the Rust verify contract for the Go 1.26 manifest assertion, then run the focused test and proceed tracer-bullet style.

- Completed the first TDD tracer bullet for the toolchain contract.
- Added a focused verify-source contract assertion that go.mod must declare Go 1.26, ran the isolated Rust test RED, then updated cockroachdb_molt/molt/go.mod to go 1.26 and got that test GREEN.
- Confirmed the duplicate error-boundary smell is real: the only retained pkg/errors usage is mysqlconv plus the go.mod/go.sum entries.
- Local `go test ./...` now fails immediately because the host only has go1.25.5 while the module now requires go >= 1.26; later Go validation will need GOTOOLCHAIN bootstrap or an installed 1.26 toolchain.
- Next: add the second failing Rust contract for pkg/errors absence, then replace that import and refresh the module graph.

- Continued execution into slice 2 and discovered the existing manifest-boundary design is wrong.
- Evidence: `go mod tidy` removed direct pkg/errors usage after mysqlconv import cleanup, but pkg/errors remains structurally required via retained verify-slice dependencies; `go mod why -m github.com/pkg/errors` points to pgconv -> github.com/cockroachdb/apd/v3, and the latest github.com/cockroachdb/errors v1.12.0 still directly requires github.com/pkg/errors.
- Also found the task-01 style go.sum string assertions are too brittle for modern module refresh: `go mod tidy` reintroduced oauth2 checksum lines even though `go mod why -m golang.org/x/oauth2` says the main module does not need it.
- Because the current execution plan assumes pkg/errors can disappear from go.mod/go.sum and that raw go.sum absence is a valid contract, the plan must be switched back to TO BE VERIFIED.
- Next for the replanning turn: redefine manifest assertions around direct requirements / actual module-graph intent, and decide whether the boundary goal is “no direct pkg/errors imports in retained source” instead of “pkg/errors absent everywhere in go.mod/go.sum”.

- Read the startup progress and confirmed the previous execution turn invalidated the plan assumptions.
- Verified the task pointer still targets story-18 task 02; this turn should inspect the task file and its linked plan, switch the plan back to TO BE VERIFIED if needed, and stop instead of continuing execution.
- Next: rewrite the plan around direct dependency boundaries and retained-source imports rather than impossible full-module-graph/go.sum absence.

- Read the task file and linked plan; the plan is already back to TO BE VERIFIED, but its execution slices still need correction.
- I am inspecting the current workspace diff, verify contract support code, and the retained Go manifest/import boundary so the rewritten plan matches actual module-graph behavior instead of brittle go.sum expectations.
- Next: rewrite the plan file to use direct-requirement and retained-source-import assertions, then stop for verification as required.

- Confirmed the aborted execution already changed real code and tests: go.mod declares Go 1.26, mysqlconv already uses cockroachdb/errors, and the Rust repo contract still contains the invalid pkg/errors manifest absence assertion.
- I am checking the exact diffs before editing the plan so the replanning document accurately describes the dirty starting point for the next execution turn.
- Next: patch the plan file to replace brittle go.sum/full-manifest absence rules with direct-requirement and retained-source-import boundaries, then stop.

- Rewrote the task-02 plan around boundaries we actually own: Go version, retained source imports, and go.mod direct requirements.
- Removed the invalid planning assumptions that treated raw go.sum text or full module-graph absence as architecture contracts; the plan now explicitly keeps task 02 in TO BE VERIFIED.
- Stop here per Ralph workflow. Next turn should review the revised plan, and only after explicit satisfaction switch it to NOW EXECUTE before touching the implementation again.

- Read startup progress, current task pointer, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skills.
- Confirmed the prior turn intentionally left story-18 task 02 in replanning state because the original execution plan made invalid assumptions about go.sum / full module-graph absence.
- Next: read the task file and its linked plan, verify whether it still ends in `TO BE VERIFIED`, and only then decide whether this turn must stop after planning changes.

- Read the task-02 file and linked plan, then validated the rewritten replanning document against the current dirty workspace.
- Confirmed the plan now matches reality: the verify module already declares Go 1.26, `mysqlconv` already removed the retained `pkg/errors` import, and the remaining bad boundary lives in the Rust contract helpers/tests that still treat raw `go.sum` and full manifest absence as architecture rules.
- Replaced the plan trailer with `NOW EXECUTE` in `.ralph/tasks/story-18-verify-http-image/02-task-upgrade-the-verify-slice-to-go-1-26-and-bump-all-dependencies_plans/2026-04-19-verify-go-1-26-dependency-upgrade-plan.md`.
- Stop here on purpose per Ralph workflow. Next turn should read the plan in `NOW EXECUTE`, load the `tdd` skill again, and start slice 1 by replacing the brittle manifest/go.sum assertions with a failing boundary-oriented Rust contract.

- Read startup progress, current task pointer, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skills.
- Confirmed the prior turn already switched story-18 task 02 into `NOW EXECUTE`; this turn should execute the plan, not replan.
- Next: read the task file and linked plan, inspect current workspace/test state, then continue TDD slices from the existing dirty state.

- Read the current task file and linked plan; confirmed the plan ends with `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Inspected workspace status and confirmed the in-progress files are exactly the expected verify manifest, source import, and Rust contract files.
- Next: read the current `ci_contract.rs` and `verify_source_contract.rs`, run the focused Rust contract, then continue with the next red-green slice from the existing partial implementation.

- Ran the focused Rust contract slice for `vendored_molt_manifest_excludes_fetch_only_dependency_families`.
- Confirmed RED at the stale boundary: the test fails because `verify_source_contract.rs` still treats raw `go.sum` text as architecture intent and trips on `golang.org/x/oauth2`.
- Next: inspect retained source imports and direct `go.mod` requirements for the fetch-only families, then patch the contract to assert those boundaries instead of `go.sum` contents.

- Patched `verify_source_contract.rs` to remove `go.sum` from the architecture boundary and to assert fetch-only families via retained source imports plus direct `go.mod` requirements.
- Verified the focused fetch-only Rust contract is GREEN.
- Observed the `pkg/errors` test now passes for the better reason that it is no longer checking indirect transitive manifest entries, but the test name/call site is still muddy.
- Next: rename that contract to the retained-source boundary we actually own, then continue with direct dependency refresh and local Go validation.

- Tightened the Rust contract naming to the real boundary: retained source imports for `github.com/pkg/errors`.
- Verified focused Rust contracts for fetch-only families, Go 1.26 declaration, and retained-source `pkg/errors` are GREEN.
- Started local Go validation with `GOTOOLCHAIN=auto go test ./...` and hit the next RED under Go 1.26: `verify/inconsistency/reporter.go:113` uses a non-constant format string with `zerolog.Event.Msgf`.
- Next: patch that reporter call to use the right zerolog API for dynamic messages, then rerun Go tests and continue fixing the first failure at a time.

- Fixed the Go 1.26 vet regressions by replacing dynamic `Msgf(...)` calls with `Msg(...)` in `mysqlconv/data_type.go` and `verify/inconsistency/reporter.go`.
- Reran `GOTOOLCHAIN=auto go test ./...`; compiler/vet is now clear, and the remaining failures are integration tests that cannot connect to expected local databases (MySQL on 3306, Cockroach/Postgres on 26257/defaultdb).
- Next: inspect the repo test orchestration (`Makefile` and related scripts) to see the supported way to provision those dependencies, then continue through the required validation lanes instead of skipping anything.

- Fixed the disposable Cockroach setup properly by exposing SQL with `--sql-addr`; host-side `psql` can now reach both source and target instances.
- Re-ran focused Go tests: `verify/rowverify` is now GREEN, proving the earlier Cockroach connection resets were an environment setup issue.
- The remaining focused Go failure is in `molttelemetry`: Cockroach v26.1 now rejects direct reads from `crdb_internal.feature_usage` with SQLSTATE 42501.
- Next: inspect `molttelemetry` implementation/tests and adjust the verification path to a permission-safe public contract that still proves telemetry reporting behavior.

- Disposable Postgres/MySQL/Cockroach test environment is now working; full retained-slice `GOTOOLCHAIN=auto go test ./...` passes with explicit env overrides.
- Key code changes beyond the Rust contract: fixed Go 1.26 vet failures by replacing dynamic `Msgf` uses with `Msg`, and updated the telemetry test to opt into Cockroach's `allow_unsafe_internals` guard before reading `crdb_internal.feature_usage`.
- Next: run the required repository lanes `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long`, then finish the task bookkeeping only if everything is green.

- All required validation lanes are green: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long` all passed.
- The retained verify slice also passes explicit `GOTOOLCHAIN=auto go test ./...` against the disposable Postgres/MySQL/Cockroach environment.
- Next: perform the final improve-code-boundaries review, update the task and plan files to reflect completion, run `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, commit all changes including `.ralph`, and push.
